title: Span Heatmap Samples
description: Small per-corpus samples for localized Marin-Qwen bpb comparison. The dashboard loads only this index at startup; each corpus sample manifest and its per-character payloads are fetched only when opened.

sets.title: Synthetic reasoning: connected components
sets.description: Native and CLRS-style connected-components examples in neutral arrow-format 5-shot ICL. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Synthetic reasoning: BFS graph search
sets.description: Native and CLRS-style BFS examples in neutral arrow-format 5-shot ICL. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Synthetic reasoning: arithmetic
sets.description: Arithmetic examples in neutral arrow-format 5-shot ICL, target-only scored. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Synthetic reasoning: Euclid GCD
sets.description: Euclid GCD examples in neutral arrow-format 5-shot ICL, target-only scored. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Synthetic reasoning: Floyd-Warshall
sets.description: Native and CLRS-style Floyd-Warshall examples in neutral arrow-format 5-shot ICL. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Synthetic numeric formats: numeric transduction (equals prompt)
sets.description: Digit-heavy copy/increment, compare/sort, range/checksum, and chunk-boundary stress tests using the base-model equals prompt format. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Synthetic numeric formats: structured records (equals prompt)
sets.description: CSV/TSV rows, IP/port rows, and JSON numeric arrays using the base-model equals prompt format. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Synthetic numeric formats: dense numeric blobs (equals prompt)
sets.description: SVG path numeric blobs, mmCIF coordinate tables, and format-preserving numeric transforms using the base-model equals prompt format. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Synthetic numeric prompt ablation: newline
sets.description: Numeric-format prompt ablation using query and target separated by a bare newline. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Synthetic numeric prompt ablation: arrow
sets.description: Numeric-format prompt ablation using an arrow-style query-to-target delimiter. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Synthetic numeric prompt ablation: equals
sets.description: Numeric-format prompt ablation using an equals-style query-to-target delimiter. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Synthetic surface forms: delimiters and tables
sets.description: TSV/CSV, rare delimiters, table rows, fixed-width columns, and indentation/tab continuations. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Synthetic surface forms: identifiers and encodings
sets.description: Package/version strings, hashes, UUIDs, bio accessions, Base64/hex, URL escapes, and JSON byte escapes. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Synthetic surface forms: machine records
sets.description: Service logs, tracebacks, compiler errors, CI logs, JSON app logs, and config manifests. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Synthetic surface forms: patch and review
sets.description: Unified diffs, file/line references, review comments, failing-test traces, commit metadata, and PR events. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Synthetic surface forms: science markup and trees
sets.description: SMILES/SDF/PDB/mmCIF/FASTA/GenBank records, BibTeX/LaTeX, XML/HTML/SVG/MathML tree closures. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Code: GitHub Python and C++
sets.description: Source-code examples from the GitHub Python and C++ slices. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Code: Verilog eval prompts and solutions
sets.description: Verilog prompt and canonical-solution slices from the formal-hardware long-tail set. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Stack v2: JSON
sets.description: Stack v2 JSON rows where Qwen is sharply ahead, including serialized payloads and encoded blobs. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Stack v2: Assembly
sets.description: Stack v2 assembly rows where Marin is often ahead on address-like and instruction-format continuations. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Stack v2: Scala and C#
sets.description: JVM/.NET-adjacent Stack v2 slices from the v0 cut. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Stack v2: Coq and Mathematica
sets.description: Formal proof and symbolic-math Stack v2 slices with very different surface forms. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Stack v2: high-gap specialty formats

sets.description: SVG/XML validation and test slices. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.description: FineWeb2 Chinese/Japanese/Russian slices to inspect script-specific behavior. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.description: OCR/ASR clean/noisy and OCR token slices where Marin is often ahead. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: OCR/Markup: TextOCR and metadata
sets.description: TextOCR strings plus OCR-VQA metadata/info JSON slices. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.description: Structured GitHub Archive event payload slices. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.description: Common Crawl WARC and WAT raw web markup slices. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.description: Table-oriented structured-text slices restored in v14. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.description: LM-eval training slices for auxiliary MMLU and GSM8K. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: MCQA/FEVER: QASC and CommonSenseQA
sets.description: Largest aggregate Marin-better MCQA slices from the corrected 32B MCQA/FEVER 5-shot run. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: MCQA/FEVER: SciQ, OpenBookQA, and ARC
sets.description: Science/open-book multiple-choice slices in corrected 5-shot whole-answer-string format. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: MCQA/FEVER: MMLU auxiliary and RACE
sets.description: Longer MCQA slices with 5-shot ICL and whole-answer-string targets in both demonstrations and held-out rows. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: MCQA/FEVER: FEVER factuality
sets.description: Evidence-conditioned FEVER factuality validation rows. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Long context: 32k validation and fixed QA
sets.description: PG19, GovReport, QuALITY, fixed QASPER answer-prompt QA, and NarrativeQA examples from the long-context 32k run. Raw-document examples are cropped to the strongest local gap; QA examples keep the answer prompt and underlined scored answer. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Long context: BookSum summaries
sets.description: BookSum validation and test rows from the clean Chapter/Summary target-only render. Examples are cropped around the scored summary target.

sets.title: Instruction tuning: LIMA train
sets.description: LIMA train rows rendered as ChatML-style target-only final assistant responses. Open dialogues with no final assistant response are skipped.

sets.title: AA proxies: professional financial/legal/patent documents
sets.description: SEC 10-K/10-Q filings plus legal, regulatory, contract, court, government-publication, and patent document slices. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.description: Materialized string-only ClinicalTrials brief summaries, detailed descriptions, and eligibility criteria. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: AA proxies: factual/evidential reasoning probes
sets.description: FEVER and VitaminC target-only slices for evidence-conditioned claim likelihood, label prediction, and paired correct-vs-contradiction scoring. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Formal/package/network formats

sets.title: Music/hardware: ABC and RTL
sets.description: Irish ABC music notation and hardware RTL Verilog slices. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.

sets.title: Prompt-format sensitivity: News Classification
sets.description: Three held-out examples for `news_classification` rendered through the 26 prompt-format templates. Rows are selected from each template by the largest Marin-worse and Qwen-worse document gaps. Token losses are smeared uniformly over token byte spans before the Marin-Qwen gap is computed.
sets.corpus: Prompt-format sensitivity suite (#6067), v2 fixed JSON continuations
